entities.description: Primary cooling water pump for the reactor system. Rated at 500 GPM at 75 PSI.
Standby redundant cooling pump. Automatically engages on PUMP-101A failure.
Motorized gate valve regulating intake flow to the reactor cooling loop.
Bypass valve for emergency flow rerouting during scheduled maintenance.
Shell-and-tube heat exchanger transferring heat from reactor coolant to secondary loop.
PT100 RTD sensor monitoring casing temperature of PUMP-101A. Range: 0-150°C.
Differential pressure sensor across the cooling loop. Triggers alarm below 30 PSI.
Electromagnetic flow meter measuring volumetric flow rate in the main cooling line.
Accelerometer detecting abnormal vibration on PUMP-101A bearing assembly.
ESD protocol SOP-ESD-01: isolates reactor coolant loop within 30 seconds of critical alarm.
Quarterly bearing lubrication and seal inspection for all centrifugal pumps.
Occurs when pump casing temperature exceeds 85°C. Risk of seal failure and coolant leak.
Low suction pressure causes vapour bubble formation, leading to impeller erosion.
ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety Management System standard.
IEC 61511 Functional Safety Standard for Safety Instrumented Systems (SIS).

relationships.context: PUMP-101A discharge connects directly to VALVE-204 which controls flow into the reactor cooling loop.
Standby pump PUMP-101B routed through bypass VALVE-205 for seamless failover.
Regulated coolant flow from VALVE-204 enters the primary side of HEAT-EXCHANGER-01.
TEMP-SENS-08 continuously monitors casing temperature of PUMP-101A, triggering alarm at 85°C.
Differential pressure sensor detects suction head loss indicative of cavitation onset.
Vibration sensor detects early-stage bearing wear on PUMP-101A to enable predictive maintenance.
Flow meter verifies that VALVE-204 is delivering the required volumetric flow to the cooling loop.
A reading above 85°C from TEMP-SENS-08 classifies the operating state as OVERHEATING-RISK.
Suction pressure drop below threshold detected by PRESS-SENS-12 raises CAVITATION-RISK alert.
Detection of OVERHEATING-RISK automatically initiates the EMERGENCY-SHUTDOWN-01 ESD sequence.
Sustained cavitation triggers EMERGENCY-SHUTDOWN-01 to prevent catastrophic impeller failure.
PM03 schedule mandates quarterly inspection and bearing lubrication for PUMP-101A.
PM03 schedule equally applies to standby pump PUMP-101B to ensure failover readiness.
The ESD procedure SOP-ESD-01 is authored and audited in compliance with ISO 45001:2018.
PRESS-SENS-12 is classified as a Safety Instrumented Function (SIF) under IEC 61511 SIL-2.
TEMP-SENS-08 is part of the Safety Instrumented System and governed by IEC 61511 SIL-2.
PUMP-101B serves as the automatic standby redundancy unit for PUMP-101A.
